blocks.body: Nouns, articles and number is an important section of English grammar. Here you will learn how to correctly use articles a/an/the, countable and uncountable nouns, as well as words to express quantity: some, any, much, many, a lot of and others.

• A / An is the indefinite article for the singular: a book, an apple.
  • The is a definite article for specific things: the sun, the book I bought.
  • Zero article — with abstract and general concepts: I love music.
  • A before a consonant sound, an before a vowel: a university, an hour.
blocks.heading: Articles

blocks.body:
• Countable - you can count: one apple, two apples, three apples.
  • Uncountable - cannot be counted: water, information, advice.
  • Uncountables do not have a plural: I need some information. (not informations).
  • To measure: a glass of water, a piece of advice.
blocks.heading: Countable and uncountable nouns

blocks.body:
• Some / Any - indefinite amount: some water, any questions?
  • Much / Many - many: much time (uncountable), many friends (countable).
  • A lot of / Lots of - a lot (universal): a lot of money, lots of people.
  • A few / A little — a little: a few books, a little sugar.
  • Few / Little - little (negative): few friends, little hope.
blocks.heading: Quantifiers

blocks.body:
Topic Level Description
A / An / The A1–A2 Definite and indefinite articles
Countable & Uncountable A1–A2 Countable and uncountable nouns
Some / Any A1–A2 Quantity in affirmatives and negatives
Much / Many / A lot of A2–B1 Expressing a large amount
A few / A little A2–B1 Expressing a small amount
Plural Nouns A1–A2 Plural nouns
blocks.heading: Topics in this section

blocks.body:
• Some — in statements: I have some money.
  • Any — in questions and objections: Do you have any money?
  • Some in questions - when we offer: Would you like some tea?
  • Any in statements - when "any": Take any seat.
blocks.heading: Some vs Any is the basic rule

blocks.body:
🧠

Start with the articles a/an/the - they are used in every sentence.

Memorize the list of uncountable nouns: water, information, advice, news, furniture.

Much/many — for questions and objections, a lot of — a universal option.

A few/a little = "a little" (positive), few/little = "little" (negative).
blocks.heading: Learning tips
